description_long: This is a dark stone sculpture depicting a seated human figure, likely of ancient Egyptian origin, wearing a traditional nemes headdress and a kilt. The figure sits upright on a blocky throne with arms resting on the knees, one hand clenched and the other open. The throne features carved reliefs on the sides, possibly with symbolic or hieroglyphic motifs. The back of the sculpture is flat and unadorned, showing the solid mass of the stone. The surface is mostly smooth but shows some weathering and minor chipping, especially on the edges. The overall form is monumental and exudes a sense of authority and permanence.
description: A dark stone sculpture of a seated human figure in ancient Egyptian style, wearing a nemes headdress and sitting on a throne with carved reliefs.
description_view_0: Frontal view showing the full figure, facial features, headdress, and both arms resting on the knees.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the profile of the figure, the arm position, and the detailed carvings on the side of the throne.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the flat, unadorned back of the sculpture and the headdress from behind.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the figure's profile, arm position, and the throne's carvings similar to the left side.
attribute_subject_matter: seated human figure in ancient Egyptian style
attribute_location_context: No visible context; background is isolated and does not indicate installation environment.
